data.title: Markforged Announces Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
WALTHAM, Mass., March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Markforged Holding Corporation (“Markforged”), the company strengthening manufacturing resiliency by enabling industrial production at the point of need, today announced its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024.

Fourth Quarter 2024 Financial Results Compared To Fourth Quarter 2023

  • Revenue was $22.4 million compared to $24.2 million.
  • Gross margin was 44.8% compared to 48.4%.
  • Non-GAAP gross margin was 46.4% compared to 49.5%.
  • Operating expenses were $25.0 million compared to $31.1 million.
  • Non-GAAP operating expenses were $19.9 million compared to $24.9 million.
  • Net loss was $11.9 million compared to net loss of $14.2 million.
  • Non-GAAP net loss was $9.0 million compared to a loss of $11.6 million.
  • Cash and cash equivalents, including restricted cash, were $53.6 million as of December 31, 2024, compared to $116.9 million as of December 31, 2023.

Full Year 2024 Financial Results Compared To Full Year 2023

  • Revenue was $85.1 million compared to $93.8 million.
  • Gross margin was 48.3% compared to 47.4%.
  • Non-GAAP gross margin was 50.1% compared to 48.6%.
  • Net cash used in operating activities was $61.3 million compared to $48.9 million.

Reconciliations of the non-GAAP financial measures provided in this press release to their most directly comparable GAAP financial measures are provided in the financial tables included at the end of this press release. An explanation of these measures and how they are calculated is also included below under the heading “Non-GAAP Financial Measures.”

“We are encouraged by the continued adoption of our next generation product line, despite the challenging market conditions that we are facing,” said Shai Terem, President and CEO of Markforged. “Furthermore, we remain excited about the pending acquisition by Nano Dimension and the incremental value that we believe it will bring to our customers on the manufacturing floor and beyond.”

Acquisition Updates

  • Nano Dimension Ltd. (“Nano Dimension”) to Acquire Markforged: On September 25, 2024, Markforged entered into a definitive agreement (the “Merger Agreement”) pursuant to which Nano Dimension will acquire all outstanding shares of Markforged in an all-cash transaction for $5.00 per share, without interest and less any applicable tax withholdings (the “Merger”). The transaction, which was unanimously approved by the Boards of Directors of both companies, is expected to close by the second quarter of 2025, subject to the satisfaction or waiver of certain closing conditions, including receipt of required regulatory approval from the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States.

No Earnings Call and Guidance

In light of the pending merger transaction with Nano Dimension announced on September 25, 2024, and as is customary during the pendency of such transactions, Markforged will not host an earnings conference call and is not providing forward-looking guidance.

About Markforged

Markforged is enabling more resilient and flexible manufacturing by bringing industrial 3D printing right to the factory floor. Our additive manufacturing platform The Digital Forge allows manufacturers to create strong, accurate parts in both metal and advanced composites. With over 10,000 customers in 70+ countries, we’re bringing on-demand industrial production to the point of need. We are headquartered in Waltham, Mass where we design the hardware, software and advanced materials that make The Digital Forge reliable and easy to use. To learn more, visit www.markforged.com. 

Non-GAAP Financial Measures

In addition to our financial results determined in accordance with U.S. generally accepted accounting principles (“GAAP”), we believe that each of non-GAAP gross margin, non-GAAP operating profit (loss) and non-GAAP net profit (loss), each a non-GAAP financial measure, is useful in evaluating the performance of our business.

These non-GAAP measures have limitations as an analytical tool. We do not, nor do we suggest that investors should, consider such non-GAAP financial measures in isolation from, or as a substitute for, financial information prepared in accordance with GAAP. Investors should also note that the non-GAAP financial measures we use may not be the same non-GAAP financial measures, and may not be calculated in the same manner, as that of other companies, including other companies in our industry.

We recommend that you review the reconciliation of these non-GAAP measures to the most directly comparable GAAP financial measures provided in the financial statement tables included below in this press release, and that you not rely on any single financial measure to evaluate our business. Additionally, to the extent that forward-looking non-GAAP financial measures are provided, they are presented on a non-GAAP basis without reconciliations of such forward-looking non-GAAP measures due to the inherent difficulty in forecasting and quantifying certain amounts that are necessary for such reconciliations.

The following are the non-GAAP financial measures referenced in this press release and presented in the tables below:

  • Non-GAAP gross margin is defined as GAAP gross profit (loss), less stock-based compensation expense, amortization, and certain non-recurring costs, divided by revenue.
  • Non-GAAP operating profit (loss) is defined as GAAP operating profit (loss) less stock-based compensation expense, amortization, and certain non-recurring costs.
  • Non-GAAP net profit (loss) is defined as GAAP net profit (loss) less stock-based compensation expense, net change in fair value of warrant liabilities and contingent earnout liabilities, amortization, and certain non-recurring costs.

Forward-Looking Statements

Any statements in this message about Markforged’s future expectations, plans and prospects, as well as any other statements regarding matters that are not historical facts, may constitute “forward-looking statements” within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995.

Such statements are subject to risks and uncertainties and actual results may differ materially from those expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements. Such forward-looking statements include statements relating to the proposed Merger between Markforged and Nano Dimension, the benefits sought to be achieved through the transaction, the anticipated timing of the consummation of the Merger, the potential effects of the transaction, the ability of Markforged and Nano Dimension to complete the transactions contemplated by the Merger Agreement, including the parties’ ability to satisfy the conditions to the consummation of the Merger contemplated thereby, Markforged’s business and expectations regarding outlook and all underlying assumptions, Markforged’s objectives, plans and strategies, operating trends in markets where Markforged operates, projections of results of operations or of financial condition and all other statements other than statements of historical fact that address activities, events or developments that Markforged intends, expects, projects, believes or anticipates will or may occur in the future. Such statements are based on management’s beliefs and assumptions made based on information currently available to management. All statements in this communication, other than statements of historical fact, are forward-looking statements that may be identified by the use of the words “outlook,” “guidance,” “expects,” “believes,” “anticipates,” “should,” “estimates,” “may,” “will,” “intends,” “projects,” “could,” “would,” “estimate,” “potential,” “continue,” “plan,” “target,” or the negative of these words or similar expressions. These forward-looking statements involve known and unknown risks and uncertainties, which may cause Markforged’s actual results and performance to be materially different from those expressed or implied in the forward-looking statements. Factors and risks that may cause Markforged’s or Nano Dimension’s actual results or performance to be materially different from those expressed or implied in the forward-looking statements include, but are not limited to: (i) the occurrence of any event, change or other circumstance that could give rise to the termination of the Merger Agreement; (ii) the effect of the announcement of the proposed transaction on the ability of Markforged to operate its business and retain and hire key personnel and to maintain favorable business relationships; (iii) the ability of the parties to consummate the proposed transaction in a timely manner or at all; (iv) the satisfaction (or waiver) of closing conditions to the consummation of the proposed transaction, including the receipt of required regulatory approvals; (v) the response of competitors, suppliers and customers to the proposed transaction; (vi) risks associated with the disruption of management’s attention from ongoing business operations due to the proposed transaction; (vii) significant costs associated with the proposed transaction; (viii) the ultimate impact of the proposed transaction between Desktop Metal, Inc. (“Desktop Metal”) and Nano Dimension, including the impact, if any, of litigation between Desktop Metal and Nano Dimension, or any other potential litigation relating to the proposed Merger between Nano Dimension and Markforged; (ix) restrictions during the pendency of the proposed transaction that may impact Markforged’s ability to pursue certain business opportunities; (x) the ability of Markforged to continue as a going concern; and (xi) other risks, uncertainties and factors discussed and described in reports filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (“SEC”) by Markforged and Nano Dimension from time to time, including those under the heading “Risk Factors” in their respective most recently filed reports on Form 10-K, 10-Q or 20-F, as applicable, and subsequent filings with the SEC.

The forward-looking statements included in this communication are made only as of the date hereof. Markforged undertakes no obligation to update any forward-looking statements to reflect subsequent events or circumstances, except as required by law.

Lotus Technology to Report Unaudited Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2024 Financial Results on April 22, 2025
data.description:
NEW YORK, March 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus” or the “Company”) , a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced that it will report its unaudited fourth quarter and full year 2024 financial results on April 22, 2025, before market opening in the United States.

The Company’s management will host an earnings conference call at 8:00 AM U.S. Eastern Time on Tuesday, April 22, 2025 (14:00 Central European Time / 20:00 China Standard Time on the same day).

There will be a live audio webcast and replay available following completion of the call on the Company’s investor relations website at https://ir.group-lotus.com/news-events/events/

For participants who wish to view the live webcast, please register at https://edge.media-server.com/mmc/p/mdbmd5h5

For participants who wish to join the call, please complete online registration prior to the scheduled call start time using the link provided below. Upon registration, participants will receive a confirmation email with conference call access information, including dial-in numbers and a unique PIN.

Participant online registration link: https://register-conf.media-server.com/register/BI6f2e717ea0604566888b1c361deb51c5

About Lotus Technology Inc.

Lotus Technology Inc. has operations across the UK, the EU and China. The Company is dedicated to delivering luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicles, with a focus on world-class R&D in next-generation automobility technologies such as electrification, digitalisation and more. For more information about Lotus Technology Inc., please visit www.group-lotus.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

data.title: Marvel Biosciences Announces Private Placement
data.description:
Calgary, Alberta--(Newsfile Corp. - March 28, 2025) - Marvel Biosciences Corp. and its wholly owned subsidiary, Marvel Biotechnology Inc. (collectively the "Company" or "Marvel"), is pleased to announce that, subject to approval of the TSX Venture Exchange (the "TSXV"), it has closed the first tranche of a non-brokered private placement of up to 12,000,000 units (the "Units") at a price of $0.125 per Unit for gross proceeds of up to $1,500,000 (the "Offering"). Each Unit will be comprised of one common share in the capital of the Company (a "Common Share") and one Common Share purchase warrant ("Warrant"). Each Warrant entitles the holder to purchase one Common Share at an exercise price of $0.175 per share at any time prior to 5:00 p.m. (Calgary time) on the date that is two (2) years from the date of the issuance of the Units; provided that if the volume weighted average trading price of the Common Shares on the TSX Venture Exchange (the "TSXV") is at least $0.30 per share for a period of ten (10) consecutive trading days (whether or not trading occurs on all such days) (the "Triggering Event"), the expiry date of the Warrants may be accelerated by the Corporation to a date that is not less than 30 days after the date that notice of such acceleration is provided to the Warrant holders, which notice may be by way of general press release (the "Accelerated Expiry Date"). If such news release is issued, all Warrants that are not exercised prior to 5:00 p.m. Calgary time on the Accelerated Expiry Date will expire immediately after such time on the Accelerated Expiry Date.

In connection with the Offering, the Company may pay a finder's fee ("Finder's Fee") in connection with the issue and sale of any or all of the ‎Units under the Offering. The Finder's Fee shall consist of a payment of up to 7% of the gross ‎proceeds of the Offering in relation to subscribers introduced by any particular finder, payable in cash, plus finder's ‎warrants ("Finder's Warrants") in an amount equal to up to 7% of the aggregate number of Units in relation to ‎subscribers introduced by any particular finder, with each Finder's Warrant being exercisable to acquire one (1) ‎common share of the Corporation at a price of $0.175 per share until two (2) years following the issuance of the Finder's Warrants.

The first tranche closing of the Offering resulted in the issuance of 8,000,000 Units for gross proceeds of $1,000,000.00. Each Unit is comprised of one Common Share in the capital of the Company and one Warrant. Also, in connection with the first tranche closing of the Offering, the Company paid finders fees of $54,950.00 in cash commission and issued 383,600 Finder's Warrants to certain finders. Each Finder's Warrant is exercisable to acquire one Common Share at a price of $0.175 per share for a period of two years from the date of issuance. Raymond James Ltd., Canaccord Genuity Corp., Acumen Capital Finance Partners Limited, Wood Gundy, Abinvest Corporation and Abingdon Capital Corp. acted as a finders in connection with the Offering.

The Offering is being made to accredited investors or to such other qualified persons under such other prospectus exemptions as the Company may approve, provided however, that the Offering will not be made to persons or pursuant to securities laws exemptions that would require either the preparation or the filing of a prospectus, offering memorandum or similar document by the Company.

The Common Shares and the Warrants are subject to a hold period and resale restriction in Canada that expires four months plus one day from the issuance of the Units (the "Hold Period") and such other restrictions as imposed under applicable securities legislation.

The proceeds of the Offering will be used for drug formulation, toxicology studies and for general working capital purposes. The Offering is subject to acceptance by the TSX Venture Exchange.

There is no material fact or material change about the Company that has not been generally disclosed.

About Marvel Biosciences Corp.

Marvel Biosciences Corp., and its wholly owned subsidiary, Marvel Biotechnology Inc., is a Calgary- based pre-clinical stage pharmaceutical development biotechnology company. The Company is developing MB-204, a novel ﬂuorinated derivative of the approved anti-Parkinson's drug Istradefylline, the only clinically approved adenosine A2a antagonist. A signiﬁcant and growing body of scientiﬁc evidence suggests drugs that block the adenosine A2a receptor, such as MB-204, could be useful in treating other neurological diseases such as autism, depression and Alzheimer's Disease. The Company is actively investigating its potential in addressing other neurodevelopmental disorders, such as Rett Syndrome and Fragile X Syndrome, to expand its therapeutic reach.

data.title: Bitcoin Well Announces At-The-Market Equity Program
data.description:
Edmonton, Alberta--(Newsfile Corp. - March 28, 2025) - Bitcoin Well Inc. (OTCQB: BCNWF) ("Bitcoin Well" or the "Company"), the non-custodial bitcoin business on a mission to enable independence, announced today that it has established an at-the-market equity program (the "ATM Program") that allows the Company to issue and sell, at its discretion, up to $5,000,000 of common shares ("Shares") to the public from time to time.

Distributions of the Shares under the ATM Program will be made pursuant to the terms of an equity distribution agreement (the "Distribution Agreement") dated March 28, 2025 between Bitcoin Well and Haywood Securities Inc. (the "Agent"). All Shares sold under the ATM Program will be sold through the TSX Venture Exchange or other recognized Canadian marketplace at prevailing market prices at the time of sale. The ATM Program will be effective until the earlier of March 28, 2027 and the completion of the issuance and sale of all of the Shares issuable pursuant to the ATM Program, subject to earlier termination by Company or the Agent in accordance with the terms of the Distribution Agreement.

The ATM Program is intended to provide the Company with additional financing flexibility should it be required in the future. The volume and timing of distributions under the ATM Program, if any, will be determined in the Company's sole discretion. As Shares distributed under the ATM program will be sold at the prevailing market price at the time of sale, prices may vary among purchasers during the term of the ATM Program.

The Company intends to use the net proceeds from the ATM Program, if any, together with the Company's current cash resources, to fund general corporate purposes, including ongoing operations and/or working capital requirements; to repay indebtedness outstanding from time to time; to complete future acquisitions; to fund research and development, intellectual property development; or for other corporate purposes.

The offering of Shares under the ATM Program is qualified by a prospectus supplement dated March 28, 2025 (the "Prospectus Supplement") to the short form base shelf prospectus dated March 6, 2025 (the "Shelf Prospectus"). Copies of the Prospectus Supplement, the Shelf Prospectus and the Distribution Agreement are available under Bitcoin Well's profile on the SEDAR+ website at www.sedarplus.ca. Alternatively, the Company or the Agent will send the Prospectus Supplement (including the Shelf Prospectus) upon request. Such requests may be made by sending an email to Haywood at ecm@haywood.com.

This news release shall not constitute an offer to sell, or the solicitation of an offer to buy, the Shares, nor shall there be any sale of these securities in any jurisdiction in which such offer, solicitation or sale would be unlawful prior to registration or qualification under the securities laws of any such jurisdiction.

The securities being referred to in this news release have not been, nor will they be, registered under the United States Securities Act of 1933, as amended (the "1933 Act"), and may not be offered or sold in the United States or to, or for the account or benefit of, U.S. persons (as defined in Regulation S under the 1933 Act) absent registration or an applicable exemption from the registration requirements. data.title: Millennial Announces Closing Private Placement for Total Proceeds of $4,929,200
data.description:
West Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - March 28, 2025) - Millennial Potash Corp. (OTCQB: MLPNF) (FSE: X0D) ("MLP", "Millennial" or the "Company") is pleased to announce that it has closed its private placement (the "Private Placement") of units (the "Units") comprised of one common share of the Company (the "Shares") and one-half share purchase warrant (each warrant being a "Warrant" or the "Warrants"). Each of the Warrants issued are exercisable for a period of two years from the date of issuance at a price of CDN$0.85 per Share. The Private Placement was first announced on March 18, 2025, and amended to increase its size on March 25, 2025.

The Private Placement consisted of 8,215,333 units at a price of $0.60 for proceeds of $4,929,200.00.

Commissions on the Private Placement totalling CDN$92,694.00 were paid in connection with the Private Placement.

The Shares in the Units, and any Shares issued by way of exercise of Warrants, are subject to a four month hold period expiring on July 28, 2025.

Proceeds of the Private Placement are to be used to fund exploration and development activities on the Company's Banio Potash Project and for general working capital purposes.

Early Warning

This Early Warning section of this news release is jointly issued by the Quaternary Group Ltd. (the "Acquiror") and the Company as required by National Instrument 62-103 - The Early Warning System and Related Take-Over Bid and Insider Reporting Issues and is issued in connection with the acquisition below of ownership of securities of the Company by The Quaternary Group Ltd. (the "Acquiror").

The Acquiror acquired 2,333,333 Units (comprised of 2,333,333 Shares and 1,666,667 Warrants) of the Private Placement.

The Acquiror now holds a total of 24,468,833 common shares and 8,166,667 share purchase warrants (of which 1,166,667 Warrants were acquired in the Private Placement) representing approximately 26.41% of the outstanding common shares on a non-diluted basis and 32.28% of the outstanding common shares on a partially diluted basis assuming exercise of all of the Acquiror's share purchase warrants including the Warrants. The Acquiror holds 7,000,000 share purchase warrants acquired in connection with a past financing.

Prior to the closing of the Private Placement, the Acquiror owned 22,135,500 common shares and 7,000,000 share purchase warrants, representing approximately 26.22% of the outstanding Shares on a non-diluted basis and 31.8% of the outstanding common shares on a partially-diluted basis assuming exercise of all of the Acquiror's share purchase warrants.

The Shares and Warrants are held by the Acquiror for investment purposes. data.title: Tesla Shares Slide on Lower Q1 Delivery Outlook Across Key Markets
data.description:
Tesla shares declined more than 3% Friday as concerns mounted that the company's first-quarter global deliveries will fall short of expectations.

A report from RBC Capital Markets estimates Q1 deliveries at 364,000 vehicles, below the consensus forecast of 398,000. The shortfall is attributed to a temporary halt in Model Y production as Tesla prepares a refreshed version of the vehicle.

The timing of the refresh may have dampened demand, with prospective buyers reportedly waiting for the updated model. RBC also pointed to the likely release of a more affordable Tesla vehicle in Q2 as another factor contributing to reduced near-term sales.

According to RBC's regional breakdown, Q1 deliveries are expected to fall 5.4% in the U.S., decline nearly 48% in China, and drop close to 20% in Europe.

Despite the weaker outlook, RBC maintained its outperform rating and $320 price target on Tesla, citing the company's long-term potential in the electric vehicle market as well as its positions in energy, software, and artificial intelligence.

Tesla shares have declined about 36% over the past three months, reflecting both delivery concerns and shifting investor sentiment.

data.title: Swedish journalist jailed in Turkey on terrorism, insult charges
data.description:
Key points:
  • Medin charged with terrorism and insulting Erdogan
  • Sweden demands consular access for Medin
  • Medin's past conflict zone reporting cited by Turkey

Swedish journalist Kaj Joakim Medin, who was in Turkey to cover protests against Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu's arrest, was jailed pending trial on terrorism charges, Turkey's Ankara Chief Public Prosecutor's Office said on Friday.

Medin was detained in Istanbul as part of an investigation launched after a protest in Stockholm in January 2023, where a mannequin resembling President Tayyip Erdogan was hung outside the city hall.

Authorities said Medin was among 15 suspects identified for organizing, promoting, or being linked to the demonstration.

Medin was charged with "insulting the president" and "membership in a terrorist organization."

His employer, Dagens ETC newspaper, had raised concerns over his whereabouts after he was unreachable for two days.

Dagens ETC Editor-in-Chief Andreas Gustavsson told Swedish TV4 that Medin had done nothing wrong and was merely being punished for doing his job as a reporter.

Turkish authorities also cited Medin's past reporting from conflict zones in Syria, Iraq and southeastern Turkey between 2014 and 2017.

He was formally arrested via video conference by an Ankara court after being questioned in Istanbul.

Sweden has asked the Turkish ambassador to Stockholm to clarify the allegations against Medin and to demand that he receive urgent access to consular services, Swedish Foreign Minister Maria Malmer Stenergard told public broadcaster SVT.

data.title: Wheat Futures Fall on Black Sea NegotiationsDaily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

  • Wheat for May delivery fell 0.7% to $5.28 1/2 a bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade on Friday, with analysts and traders anticipating that negotiations to temporarily halt fighting will allow export shipments to pass through the Black Sea.
  • Soybeans for May delivery rose 0.5% to $10.17 1/2 a bushel.
  • Corn for May delivery rose 0.7% to $4.56 1/4 a bushel.

HIGHLIGHTS

Wheat in Retreat: The decline in wheat prices led the CBOT lower as negotiations to halt hostilities between Russia and Ukraine weigh on prices. "Though the resumption of the grain accord brokered by Turkey is uncertain, the possibility of increased Black Sea output and exports is also seen as a negative price driver, especially towards wheat," Ocean State Research's Joel Karlin said. The Black Sea Grain Initiative came into force in July 2022, and lasted for about a year.

Fear Factor: Worries about how reciprocal tariffs expected on April 2 will affect demand is weighing on the agricultural complex. "The uncertainty of it all has fear as the dominant factor in the markets heading into the weekend," said Arlan Suderman of StoneX in a note. In particular, Suderman is watching for retaliatory tariffs from Mexico, which would have a big effect on U.S. grain exports. "Retaliatory tariffs by Mexico on Ag commodities could crush demand for vital commodities."

INSIGHT

Drought May Ease: Data tracking dryness in U.S. crop-growing areas is gaining importance with analysts and traders, with extreme drought conditions spreading in the Northern Plains and in Texas, according to data from the U.S. Drought Monitor on Thursday. While the data show expanded areas of drought across the Corn Belt, rainfall is expected to hit the Eastern Corn Belt soon. "7-Day precipitation accumulation has been boosted to 2-4 inches east of the Mississippi River," Price Futures Group's Daniel Flynn said in a note. "This matches what's needed to ease/end drought there, and so is viewed as beneficial."

Unnerving Data: A hotter-than-expected February core PCE reading sparked new concerns about stagflation and the role tariffs may play going forward. "We are moving in the wrong direction and the concern is that tariffs threaten higher prices, which mean the inflation prints are going to remain hot," ING Economics said in a note. "This will constrain the Fed's ability to deliver further interest rate cuts." These concerns have pushed commodity futures into the red across the board, except for precious metals and copper, with gold surging above the $3,100 per troy ounce.

AHEAD

  • The USDA will release its weekly Grain Export Inspections report at 11 a.m. ET Monday.
  • The USDA will release its yearly Prospective Planting report at noon ET Monday.
  • The USDA will release its quarterly Grain Stocks report at noon ET Monday.

Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com

data.title: STRATUS PROPERTIES INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Stratus Properties Inc., a real estate development and management company primarily operating in the Austin, Texas area, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report highlights a significant financial turnaround, strategic initiatives aimed at long-term growth, and the challenges the company faces in the current economic environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: Stratus Properties Inc. reported total revenues of $54.183 million, a substantial increase from the previous year's $17.270 million. This growth was driven by higher performance in both Real Estate Operations and Leasing Operations.

Operating Loss: The company recorded an operating loss of $(2.155) million, a marked improvement from the previous year's operating loss of $(16.946) million, indicating better cost management and increased revenues.

Net Income (Loss) and Total Comprehensive Income (Loss) Attributable to Common Stockholders: Stratus achieved a net income of $1.956 million, compared to a loss of $(14.807) million in the prior year, highlighting a significant turnaround in profitability.

Basic and Diluted Net Income (Loss) Per Share Attributable to Common Stockholders: Both basic and diluted net income per share were $0.24, compared to $(1.85) in the previous year, reflecting improved earnings performance.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Stratus Properties Inc. generated revenue from two main segments: Real Estate Operations, which brought in $34.9 million, and Leasing Operations, contributing $19.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2024.

Geographical Performance: The company primarily operates in the Austin, Texas area and other select markets in Texas. Any significant economic downturn in these regions could potentially impact Stratus' business operations.

Sales Units: During 2024, Stratus sold multiple real estate units, including one Amarra Drive Phase III lot and five Amarra Villas homes, contributing to the company's operational performance.

New Production Launches: The company continued the development of the Holden Hills Phase 1 Project, which is part of its final large residential development within the Barton Creek community in Austin, Texas.

Future Outlook: Stratus is focused on the development and sale of multi-family and single-family residential and commercial real estate properties in the Austin, Texas area and other select markets in Texas. The company is also managing its partnerships and construction projects to optimize returns and manage risks associated with market conditions.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Partnerships: Stratus Properties Inc. has engaged in several strategic partnerships for development projects, including Holden Hills, L.P., The Saint George Apartments, L.P., Stratus Block 150, L.P., The Saint June, L.P., and Stratus Kingwood Place, L.P. These partnerships are aimed at developing residential and commercial real estate in Texas, with Stratus holding significant equity interests and potential for increased returns based on performance hurdles.

Capital Management: Stratus has actively managed its capital through various debt arrangements, including construction loans and credit facilities, to finance its development projects. The company has refinanced several loans to improve terms and extend maturities, such as the Kingwood Place loan and the Lantana Place construction loan. Additionally, Stratus has engaged in share repurchase programs, including a $10 million program completed in 2022 and a new $5 million program approved in 2023. The company also declared a special cash dividend of $4.67 per share in 2022.

Future Outlook: Stratus plans to continue its strategic focus on real estate development in Texas, leveraging its partnerships and refinancing efforts to optimize capital structure and enhance returns. The company aims to maintain compliance with financial covenants and explore opportunities for further capital investment, while managing its debt-to-asset ratio and maintaining a strong liquidity position. Future initiatives may include additional share repurchases and dividend payments, subject to lender approvals.

Challenges and Risks

Market and Economic Risks: The company faces significant challenges due to the decline in demand and prices for residential real estate in the Austin area, exacerbated by high inflation and interest rates. The enactment of Texas Senate Bill 2038 (the ETJ Law) in 2023 introduces regulatory uncertainty, as ongoing litigation may affect the company's development plans and property valuations.

Operational Risks: Stratus is exposed to risks from its reliance on joint ventures for capital, which may lead to conflicts of interest or financial obligations if partners default. The company has experienced increased costs due to inflation, higher borrowing costs, and supply chain constraints, which may continue to impact profitability and cash flow. Additionally, operational risks include potential delays and cost overruns in development projects, labor shortages, and increased construction costs.

Environmental and Catastrophic Risks: The company also faces risks from adverse weather conditions and other catastrophic events, which may be amplified by climate change and could disrupt operations or damage properties.

Management Strategies: Management acknowledges the challenges posed by elevated inflation and interest rates, which have delayed projects and impacted the ability to raise capital. The company is adjusting its development plans in response to the ETJ Law and is incurring costs aligned with revised plans, subject to the risk of the law being invalidated. Management is also focused on maintaining strong relationships with key tenants to mitigate risks in leasing operations.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks from variable interest rate debt, which could increase interest costs if rates rise. The company typically does not hedge against interest rate changes, except for specific loans where required by lenders. Additionally, the company faces risks from potential changes in U.S. trade policies, such as tariffs on lumber imports, which could increase construction costs.

SEC Filing: STRATUS PROPERTIES INC [ STRS ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: PEOPLES FINANCIAL SERVICES CORP. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Peoples Financial Services Corp., a bank holding company operating through its subsidiary Peoples Security Bank and Trust Company, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment. The company, which serves retail and commercial customers through 39 full-service community banking offices across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and New York, has detailed significant developments and future outlooks in its latest filing.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $211.46 million, reflecting a significant increase from the previous year due to higher interest and fees on loans.
  • Net Interest Income: $115.99 million, showing growth driven by increased interest income despite higher interest expenses.
  • Net Income: $8.50 million, a decrease from the previous year, impacted by higher noninterest expenses and provision for credit losses.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share: $1.00, reflecting the impact of increased shares outstanding due to the acquisition of FNCB Bancorp, Inc.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $0.99, consistent with the basic earnings per share, indicating minimal dilution effects.

Business Highlights

  • Nature of Operations: Peoples Financial Services Corp. operates as a bank holding company providing a full range of financial services through its subsidiary, Peoples Security Bank and Trust Company. The company serves retail and commercial customers through 39 full-service community banking offices across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and New York.
  • Product Lines: The Bank's primary products include loans to small and medium-sized businesses, equipment financing and leasing, one-to-four family residential mortgages, and consumer loans. Deposit products offered include checking accounts, savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates in several counties across Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and New York, facing competition from both local and national financial institutions. The competitive landscape is influenced by changes in legal and regulatory guidelines and economic conditions.
  • Business Combination: On July 1, 2024, Peoples Financial Services Corp. completed the acquisition of FNCB Bancorp, Inc., expanding its branch network and market share in northeastern Pennsylvania. This merger is expected to create meaningful value for shareholders and enhance the company's market presence.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to leverage the synergies from the FNCB acquisition to enhance operational efficiencies and expand its customer base. The integration of FNCB is expected to provide increased trading liquidity and dividends for shareholders.
  • Operational Strategy: The company focuses on prudent underwriting practices, conservative debt service coverage, and loan-to-value ratios to manage credit risk effectively. It also emphasizes lending to seasoned real estate owners and managers, often with personal guarantees of repayment.
  • Regulatory Environment: Peoples Financial Services Corp. and its subsidiaries are subject to regulations from federal and state agencies, undergoing periodic examinations to ensure compliance with banking laws and regulations.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company completed the acquisition of FNCB Bancorp, Inc., expanding its branch network and market share in northeastern Pennsylvania. This strategic merger was aimed at enhancing shareholder value, increasing trading liquidity, and providing a stronger cultural alignment with a commitment to shareholders, customers, employees, and communities.
  • Capital Management: The company issued 2,935,456 shares of common stock valued at $45.54 per share as part of the merger consideration for FNCB Bancorp, Inc. Additionally, the company declared cash dividends of $2.06 per common share in 2024, up from $1.64 in 2023. The company also engaged in stock compensation activities, including the issuance of restricted stock and retirement of shares.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue integrating FNCB's operations to realize synergies and economies of scale. It will focus on maintaining a strong capital position to support future growth and shareholder returns, while also exploring opportunities for further strategic acquisitions and expansions.

Challenges and Risks

The company faces significant interest rate risk (IRR) due to its lending, investing, and deposit-gathering activities. This risk is primarily associated with changes in interest rates, which can affect the economic value of assets, liabilities, and off-balance sheet items. A high degree of IRR may lead to lower earnings, impaired liquidity, and capital positions, increasing the risk of insolvency. The Asset Liability Committee (ALCO) is tasked with managing this risk by establishing policy guidelines and regularly reporting to the Board of Directors. The company uses various financial instruments, including interest rate swaps, caps, and floors, to manage IRR.

The ALCO employs income simulation to measure the impact of interest rate shifts on net interest income over 24-month and 60-month horizons. As of December 31, 2024, the company's exposure to changing interest rates remained within established tolerance levels, although a sustained falling rate environment poses the greatest potential risk to net interest income over the longer term. The company's interest rate risk position was less asset-sensitive at the end of 2024 compared to 2023, due to changes in the balance sheet mix and a decrease in floating rate assets. The ALCO regularly reviews interest rate shift scenarios, including steepening or flattening yield curves and parallel changes in interest rates up to 400 basis points, to evaluate exposure and develop strategies to manage this risk. The simulation model indicates that a 100 basis point increase in interest rates would result in a 0.2% decrease in projected net interest income for the 12 months ending December 31, 2024.

SEC Filing: PEOPLES FINANCIAL SERVICES CORP. [ PFIS ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: NU RIDE INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Nu Ride Inc., formerly known as Lordstown Motors Corp., has released its 2024 10-K report, detailing the company's financial and operational performance following its emergence from Chapter 11 bankruptcy. The report highlights significant improvements in financial metrics, strategic initiatives aimed at future growth, and the challenges and risks the company faces in its new phase of operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $0 million, reflecting the cessation of operations and sales following the Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing.
  • Cost of Sales: $0 million, as there were no sales or production activities during the year ended December 31, 2024.
  • Loss from Operations: $(10.9) million, significantly reduced from the previous year's loss of $(348.9) million due to the cessation of operations and reduced expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(8.1) million, a substantial improvement from the $(343.1) million net loss in 2023, primarily due to the absence of impairment charges and reduced operating expenses.
  • Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders: $(10.8) million, compared to $(345.6) million in 2023, reflecting the impact of accrued preferred stock dividends.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(0.67), improved from $(21.67) in 2023, due to the reduced net loss and the impact of the reverse stock split.

Business Highlights

  • Bankruptcy Filing and Asset Sale: On June 27, 2023, Lordstown Motors Corp. filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, ceasing production and sales of its flagship vehicle, the Endurance. The company entered into an Asset Purchase Agreement with LandX Motors Inc. to sell assets related to electric light-duty vehicles, which closed on October 27, 2023, for $10.2 million.
  • Post-Bankruptcy Operations: Following emergence from bankruptcy in March 2024, the company, now named Nu Ride Inc., focuses on claims administration, litigation, and exploring potential business opportunities. The company has no full-time employees, with executive management provided by M3 Partners.
  • Foxconn Litigation: The company is engaged in litigation against Foxconn for alleged fraudulent and tortious conduct, with significant claims surviving a motion to dismiss. The outcome of this litigation could impact the company's financial recovery.
  • Production and Sales Cessation: Production of the Endurance vehicle ended in June 2023, with only 35 vehicles sold in the first nine months of 2023. The company repurchased most of these vehicles as part of the bankruptcy proceedings.
  • Future Business Opportunities: The company may explore strategic alternatives or business combinations to create value, although no specific agreements or discussions are currently in place.
  • Operational Focus: The company's primary operations include resolving bankruptcy claims, pursuing litigation, and identifying potential transactions to leverage its net operating loss carryforwards.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Reorganization and New Board: Nu Ride Inc. emerged from Chapter 11 bankruptcy on March 14, 2024, under a reorganization plan that included the appointment of a new Board of Directors and the termination of all remaining full-time employees.
  • Capital Management: Upon emergence from bankruptcy, Nu Ride Inc. had cash and cash equivalents of approximately $23.1 million and short-term investments of approximately $6.4 million, excluding restricted short-term investments of approximately $23.4 million. The company established a $45 million reserve for allowed and disputed claims of general unsecured creditors. The company also issued Series A Convertible Preferred Stock to Foxconn, which remains outstanding and unimpaired, with accrued dividends of $2.7 million for 2024.
  • Future Outlook: Nu Ride Inc. plans to explore potential business opportunities, including strategic alternatives or business combinations, to preserve the value of its net operating losses (NOLs). The company anticipates that the prosecution of claims and causes of action and the evaluation and pursuit of potential strategic alternatives will be costly, complex, and risky. The outcome of the Foxconn Litigation and the resolution of significant contingent claims will be critical to the company's future liquidity and capital resources.

Challenges and Risks

  • Limited Operations and Assets: The company faces significant risks due to its limited operations and assets, which may be exhausted without additional funding or successful litigation outcomes. The company's financial condition is precarious, with no significant income-generating assets and reliance on cash reserves and potential litigation recoveries.
  • Foxconn Litigation: The Foxconn Litigation is a major focus, but its outcome is uncertain and could result in substantial expenses without guaranteed recoveries.
  • Net Operating Loss Carryforwards (NOLs): The company's ability to utilize its NOLs is uncertain and may be limited by ownership changes or IRS challenges.
  • Dependence on New Management: Operational risks include dependence on a new Board of Directors and management team, with challenges in attracting and retaining qualified personnel. The company relies heavily on consultants and third-party service providers, which poses risks if their services are lost or disrupted.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company may face significant uninsured losses due to reduced insurance coverage post-bankruptcy. Changes in laws or regulations could adversely affect the company's operations and financial condition.
  • Financial Stability: The company's financial results are not comparable to historical performance due to the restructuring and bankruptcy proceedings. The company's liquidity is dependent on resolving claims and realizing value from litigation and remaining assets.

SEC Filing: NU RIDE INC. [ NRDE ] - 10-K - Mar. 28, 2025

data.title: Lifeloc Technologies, Inc SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Lifeloc Technologies, Inc., a Colorado-based developer, manufacturer, and marketer of portable hand-held and fixed station breathalyzers and related accessories, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenues: $8.54 million, a decrease of 9% from the previous year, primarily due to satisfying pent-up demand and inflationary pressures on customer budgets.

Gross Profit: $3.45 million, a decrease of 15% from the previous year, impacted by lower product sales and inflationary effects on costs.

Operating (Loss): $(1.41) million, compared to an operating loss of $(191,135) in the previous year, driven by increased research and development expenses and sales and marketing efforts.

Net Income (Loss): $(1.05) million, a significant decrease from a net income of $205,614 in the previous year, due to changes in gross profit and increased operating expenses.

Net Income (Loss) Per Share, Basic: $(0.41), compared to $0.08 in the previous year, reflecting the overall decline in net income.

Business Highlights

Business Overview: Lifeloc Technologies, Inc. operates in major segments of the breath alcohol testing instrument market, including law enforcement, workplace, corrections, OEM, and consumer markets. The company's primary products include the FC Series and Workplace Series of portable breath alcohol testers, which are DOT approved and designed for law enforcement and workplace alcohol testing programs. Lifeloc also offers supplies, accessories, services, and training to support customers' alcohol testing programs.

New Product Developments: Lifeloc is working on the commercialization of SpinDx™, a technology for the detection of drugs of abuse, which is expected to revolutionize real-time screening for high abuse drugs. The company plans to introduce this product via beta testing in 2025, with a sales-ready device anticipated in the first half of 2026.

Product Innovations: In 2019, Lifeloc received DOT approval for the LX9 and LT7 base unit alcohol breathalyzers, which have been updated and received further approval in December 2021. The company also introduced the EASYCAL® G2, an automatic calibration station with broader capabilities.

Geographical Performance: Lifeloc sells its products globally through distributors and directly to users, with over 90% of international sales made by local distributors. The company has sold instruments to customers in over 65 countries on six continents.

Future Outlook: Lifeloc anticipates growth in its breath alcohol testing business and plans to expand into new growth areas with the commercialization of new sensing and measurement products. The company expects the introduction of SpinDx™ to improve revenues in 2026 and beyond.

Operational Challenges: The company faced supply chain disruptions in 2023, leading to larger inventories to accommodate longer lead times. Inflationary pressures have increased the cost of raw materials, labor, and freight.

Training and Education: Lifeloc offers comprehensive training programs through Lifeloc University, which includes online training modules and direct training via webcam. The company acquired Superior Training Solutions, Inc. in 2014 to enhance its training offerings.

Real Property: Lifeloc owns its corporate headquarters in Wheat Ridge, Colorado, and leases a portion of the space to a tenant. The company intends to continue leasing the space but may expand its operations into the leased area in the future.

Strategic Initiatives

Strategic Initiatives: Lifeloc Technologies is focusing on expanding its product offerings and market reach. The company is committed to growing its breath alcohol testing business and anticipates the commercialization of new sensing and measurement products, such as the SpinDx technology for drug detection, which is expected to be commercialized in 2026. Additionally, the company is investing in research and development to refine existing products and develop new ones, with a significant focus on the SpinDx platform.

Capital Management: In 2024, Lifeloc Technologies issued a $750,000 subordinated debenture with an 8.25% interest rate, including warrants for 62,500 shares of common stock. The company also completed a private placement of 210,000 shares of common stock, raising $798,000. The term loan secured by a first mortgage on the company's building is being serviced with regular payments. The company maintains a healthy level of sales and marketing expenses to support its growth strategy.

Future Outlook: Lifeloc Technologies expects to see improved revenues in 2026 with the introduction of SpinDx. The company plans to focus on growing sales, increasing gross profits, and enhancing research and development efforts. It anticipates that cash resources will be sufficient to fund operations for the next twelve months under the current operating plan. The company is also exploring opportunities to expand its product offerings and market presence, with a particular emphasis on completing the SpinDx development.

Challenges and Risks

Challenges and Risks: Lifeloc Technologies, Inc. faces significant challenges in its business operations, primarily due to its reliance on a limited number of suppliers for key components such as fuel cells. Any disruption in the supply chain could severely impact production. Additionally, the company operates in a highly competitive market with larger competitors possessing greater resources, which could affect its market position.

Development and Commercialization Risks: There is no assurance that efforts to develop a marijuana breathalyzer will be successful or that significant sales will result if successful.

Economic and Trade Risks: Global economic conditions and potential shifts in U.S. trade policy, including tariffs, could negatively impact the company's operations and supply chain.

Geopolitical Risks: International conflicts and geopolitical instability may disrupt operations and supply chains.

Customer Concentration Risks: A significant portion of revenues is derived from a small number of customers, and the loss of any major customer could adversely affect revenues.

Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to extensive regulatory oversight, and failure to obtain necessary approvals could harm revenues.

Intellectual Property Risks: The company faces risks related to patent infringement and the protection of its intellectual property.

Market Risks: The company's stock lacks a significant trading market, which could lead to volatility and affect investor perceptions.

SpinDx Development: The company is investing heavily in the development of SpinDx technology for drug detection, with commercialization expected in 2026. This represents a significant opportunity but also a risk if market acceptance is delayed.

Supply Chain and Inflation: Supply chain disruptions and inflationary pressures have increased costs, impacting profitability. The company is taking steps to mitigate these effects, including sourcing new vendors and increasing prices.

Financial Performance: The company experienced a net loss in 2024 due to decreased sales and increased costs. Management is focused on growing sales and increasing research and development efforts to support long-term growth.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates and interest rates, which could adversely affect financial results. Additionally, changes in U.S. trade relations, particularly tariffs, could impact the availability of materials and international market opportunities.
